title: Online vs Traditional Classroom Learning: Student Performance Meta-Analysis
abstract: This meta-analysis of 156 studies covering 89,000 students compared learning outcomes between online and traditional classroom instruction. Online learning showed equivalent results to classroom learning for theoretical subjects, with no significant difference in test scores (p=0.23). However, hands-on subjects like laboratory sciences showed 15% lower performance in online formats. Student engagement was 18% lower in online settings, but completion rates were actually 8% higher due to increased flexibility. The study found that synchronous online classes (live video) performed better than asynchronous (recorded) by 12%. Socioeconomic factors significantly influenced online learning success, with students from higher-income families showing better adaptation to digital formats.
